scope: Seed state-level rental application fee and tenant screening charge rules for the V3 pre-application checker.
usage_note: Use for risk flagging and questions-to-ask output. Do not present as legal advice.

rules.cap_formula: Fee may not exceed actual out-of-pocket screening cost plus reasonable processing time; statutory base cap is $30 and may be adjusted annually by CPI from January 1, 1998.
refund_required_when: Fee collected but application is not actually considered under the ordered application process.
Landlord or agent does not obtain a consumer credit report or personal reference check; unused amount must be returned.
disclosure_required: Written screening criteria with the application form.
Receipt itemizing out-of-pocket expenses and time spent.
rules.portable_screening_report_rule: Landlord may accept a reusable screening report under California Civil Code section 1950.1; this seed rule does not treat acceptance as mandatory.
exceptions: The $30 amount is a base statutory amount, not the current CPI-adjusted dollar ceiling.
rules.product_risk_note: If the entered fee is materially above the CPI-adjusted cap or no unit is available, flag as ask-before-paying.

rules.cap_formula: Background and credit check fees may not exceed the actual cost or $20, whichever is less.
disclosure_required: Landlord must provide the applicant a copy of the background or credit check and the receipt or invoice from the entity conducting the check.
rules.portable_screening_report_rule: Fee must be waived if the potential tenant provides a background check or credit check conducted within the past 30 days.
exceptions: Cooperative housing corporations have special rules.
rules.product_risk_note: If a NY applicant is asked for more than $20 for an application/background/credit check, flag as high ask-before-paying risk.
rules.source_citation: New York Real Property Law section 238-a

rules.cap_formula: Charge may cover costs incurred in obtaining a tenant screening report, or actual screening costs, but may not exceed customary screening-service costs in the general area.
disclosure_required: Written or posted notice of screening information accessed.
Criteria that may result in denial.
Consumer reporting agency details if a consumer report is used.
Whether comprehensive reusable tenant screening reports are accepted.
rules.portable_screening_report_rule: Landlord website must state whether the landlord accepts a comprehensive reusable tenant screening report.
rules.product_risk_note: If screening criteria or reusable-report policy is not disclosed before payment, flag as ask-before-paying.

rules.cap_formula: Charge must not exceed the landlord's average actual screening cost or customary screening-company or credit-reporting-agency cost for comparable screening.
refund_required_when: Vacant dwelling unit is filled before screening the applicant.
Screening has not been conducted or ordered before the applicant withdraws the application in writing.
disclosure_required: Renter liability insurance requirement if applicable.
Approximate availability and applications under consideration.
exceptions: Only one applicant screening charge may be required within a 60-day period for the same landlord.
rules.product_risk_note: If the applicant has already paid this landlord in the last 60 days or no availability context is disclosed, flag as ask-before-paying.

rules.cap_formula: Fee must be used entirely to cover the landlord's costs in processing the rental application.
refund_required_when: Unused portion of the application fee should be refunded with good-faith effort within 20 days.
disclosure_required: Disclosure of anticipated expenses or itemization of actual expenses.
rules.product_risk_note: If the fee appears to include non-screening profit or no expense disclosure is provided, flag as ask-before-paying.

rules.cap_formula: Application fee may not exceed $50, exclusive of actual out-of-pocket third-party background, credit, or other pre-occupancy check expenses. HUD-regulated units use a $32 cap, exclusive of those third-party costs.
refund_required_when: Application deposit rules require refund of sums above actual expenses and damages when the applicant fails to rent or is rejected.
exceptions: HUD-regulated public or regulated housing unit cap is $32 before third-party costs.
rules.product_risk_note: If a VA application fee exceeds $50 before third-party pass-through costs, flag as ask-before-paying.

rules.cap_formula: Application fee is defined as a nonrefundable sum to offset screening costs; seed source does not provide a dollar cap.
refund_required_when: If the landlord rejects an applicant and did not make required tenant selection criteria notice available, the landlord must return the application fee and any application deposit.
disclosure_required: Tenant selection criteria and grounds for denial must be made available when the application is provided.
rules.product_risk_note: In TX, the strongest pre-apply question is whether written tenant selection criteria were made available before payment.

rules.cap_formula: Massachusetts restricts amounts charged at or before tenancy commencement and has special broker/finder fee rules; do not reduce to a single application-fee cap without jurisdiction review.
disclosure_required: Broker/finder fee responsibility and agency relationship should be clarified before payment.
exceptions: Licensed broker/finder-fee scenarios require separate treatment from landlord application fees.
rules.product_risk_note: For MA, show 'ask before paying' if the fee is labeled application, broker, finder, holding, or admin fee and the payer/agent relationship is unclear.
rules.source_citation: Mass.gov apartment finder fees, broker fees, and signing a lease guidance
